results.rendered_html:
vy-ut-√pad Ā. -padyate (aor. vyud-apādi), to proceed from, arise, originate, have origin or derivation (esp. in gram.), be derived (from a root &c.),  Śiś. x, 23 ;
(P.) to come back (from sea, cf. vy-utthā),  Divyāv. ;
to resist (?),  ib.  :
Caus. -pādayati, to cause to arise or come forth, produce, cause,  BhP. ;
(in gram.) to derive, trace back to a root &c.,  Śaṃk. ;
to discuss in detail,  Hcar.  :
Desid. See vy-utpitsu, col. 2.

results.rendered_html:
vyutpad 4 A.
1 To be produced from, originate in.
2 To be derived from (a root &c.).
3 To become proficient in or conversautconversant with.
— Caus.
1 To produce, cause.
2 To derive, trace to a root (as a word); brahmaśabdasya vyutpādyamānasya  Ś. B.
